markets.name: Vienna
markets.registration: Ortstaxe tax account (VIETour) mandatory for every host; renting beyond 90 nights/yr additionally requires a §129(1a) exemption permit — unobtainable inside Wohnzonen
markets.cap: 90 nights per calendar year without a permit (since 1 Jul 2024); primary-residence home-sharing within the cap is permit-free
markets.tax: Ortstaxe 5% of net accommodation revenue since 1 Jul 2026 (was 3.2%; rises to 8% on 1 Jul 2027)
markets.process: Open a tax account via the VIETour online application; remit Ortstaxe by the 15th of the month following each stay; file the annual declaration by 15 February. For >90 nights/yr, apply to the city (MA 37) for a §129(1a) Ausnahmebewilligung — granted for max 5 years and only for addresses outside Wohnzonen.
markets.whoCanHost: Home-sharers renting their primary residence up to 90 nights (without abandoning it) need no permit. Investors and second-home owners need the exemption permit and a non-Wohnzone address.
markets.zones: Wohnzonen (covering much of central Vienna): tourist short-term rental effectively barred — exemption permits are not granted there, nor in Grünland/Kleingarten land categories.
markets.penalties: Merely offering an apartment beyond 90 nights/yr without the permit is punishable — fines up to €50,000.
markets.enforcement: Enforced by the city building authority (MA 37) under the tightened 2024 Bauordnung regime; Ortstaxe compliance tracked via VIETour accounts.

history.change: Ortstaxe raised from 3.2% to 5% of net accommodation revenue; a further rise to 8% is legislated for 1 Jul 2027.
90-night annual cap and §129(1a) exemption-permit regime in force; offering beyond the cap without a permit fined up to €50,000.

markets.verdict: Home-sharing is safe; the investor play needs a non-Wohnzone address plus a 5-year exemption permit — and the tourist tax just jumped to 5%.

markets.name: Salzburg
markets.registration: Permit required; new STRs heavily restricted in the city
markets.cap: Apartment STRs largely prohibited without dedicated permit
markets.verdict: Apartment STRs are effectively closed to newcomers without a dedicated permit.

markets.name: Berlin
markets.registration: Registration number (Registriernummer) mandatory on listings; Zweckentfremdung permit for non-primary homes
markets.cap: Primary residence: unlimited with registration; secondary: max 90 days/yr with permit
markets.tax: City Tax 5% of net accommodation price
markets.verdict: Primary residences work with a registration number; second homes face a hard permit wall.

markets.name: Munich
markets.registration: Zweckentfremdung rules; permit needed beyond 8 weeks/yr letting
markets.cap: Max 8 weeks/yr without permit
markets.tax: No city tourist tax (Bavaria); VAT rules apply
markets.verdict: Eight weeks a year without a permit — beyond that the city says no.

markets.name: Barcelona
markets.registration: HUT licence required; NO new licences issued
markets.cap: City announced phase-out of all tourist flats by 2028
markets.tax: Regional tourist tax + city surcharge per night
markets.verdict: No new licences and an announced phase-out: the market is closing, not tightening.

markets.name: Madrid
markets.registration: VUT licence required; independent-entrance rule restricts most apartments in central zones
markets.cap: Zone-dependent; central district heavily restricted
markets.verdict: Central Madrid demands an independent entrance most apartments simply don't have.

markets.name: Lisbon
markets.registration: Alojamento Local (AL) registration; new registrations suspended in containment zones
markets.cap: Zone containment; existing ALs subject to periodic review
markets.tax: Municipal tourist tax per night (capped nights)
markets.verdict: New AL registrations frozen in containment zones; existing licences under review.

markets.name: Porto
markets.registration: AL registration required; containment zones in the historic centre
markets.cap: Zone-dependent
markets.verdict: Registration works outside the historic-centre containment zones — check the map first.

markets.name: Paris
markets.registration: Mandatory registration number on all listings; change-of-use rules for secondary residences
markets.cap: Primary residence max 120 nights/yr (90 from 2025 national law option)
markets.tax: Taxe de séjour per person per night
markets.verdict: Primary residences up to 120 nights with registration; second homes need a change-of-use trade.

markets.name: Amsterdam
markets.registration: National registration number mandatory on every listing; vakantieverhuur permit (vergunning) required; every individual stay must be reported to the city via the RTV system before it starts
markets.cap: 30 nights/yr citywide — CUT to 15 nights/yr in the busiest central districts (Burgwallen-Nieuwe Zijds, Grachtengordel W/Z, Jordaan, Nieuwmarkt, Haarlemmerbuurt, De Weteringschans, Oude Pijp) since 1 Apr 2026; max 4 guests
markets.tax: 12.5% tourist tax on the room price ex-VAT (unchanged for 2026) — but national VAT on stays rises 9%→21% in 2026, pushing total tax on a booking to roughly a third
markets.process: Register for the national registration number, apply for the vakantieverhuur permit, then report each rental (dates + nights) through the RTV portal before guests arrive. Skipping a single stay report is itself a violation.
markets.whoCanHost: Only the resident of the home — you must live there and be registered at the address. Renting an investment unit as vakantieverhuur is not possible; that requires a hotel/B&B track with its own permits.
markets.zones: City-wide regime plus the 2026 neighbourhood tier: seven central areas now carry the 15-night cap; the rest of the city keeps 30. Council direction of travel is further tightening.
markets.penalties: Fines under the Amsterdam Housing Ordinance 2024 per violation, plus conditional penalty orders (dwangsom) for repeat offenders; unreported stays and cap breaches are the classic triggers.
markets.enforcement: Among Europe's most active: per-stay RTV reports give the city a live ledger to enforce against, and platforms enforce the registration number at listing level.

history.change: Night cap halved from 30 to 15 in seven central neighbourhoods (Centre + Oude Pijp) under the tightened vakantieverhuur rules.
National VAT on accommodation rises 9% → 21%; Amsterdam tourist tax holds at 12.5% ex-VAT.

markets.verdict: Hosting your own home works — 30 nights, or just 15 in the centre since April. As a business market it's closed, and both the caps and the taxes are moving against you.

markets.name: London
markets.registration: No registration yet; national scheme announced/in progress
markets.cap: Max 90 nights/yr short-term letting without planning permission
markets.verdict: Ninety nights without planning permission, registration scheme incoming — direction of travel is tighter.

markets.name: Dublin
markets.registration: RTB/Fáilte Ireland STR register; planning permission needed in rent-pressure zones for non-primary
markets.cap: Primary residence: 90 days/yr while owner away
markets.verdict: Rent-pressure zones need planning permission for anything beyond hosting your own spare room.

markets.name: Prague
markets.registration: Trade licence + guest reporting; new national registry legislation in progress
markets.cap: No hard cap (as seeded)
markets.verdict: Trade licence plus guest reporting — bureaucratic but genuinely open, for now.

markets.name: Budapest
markets.registration: NTAK registration mandatory; District VI banned new STRs (referendum)
markets.cap: District-level powers; Terézváros ban from 2026
markets.verdict: District-by-district powers: fine in most of the city, banned outright in District VI from 2026.

markets.name: Rome
markets.registration: CIN national code mandatory on listings; SCIA to the municipality
markets.cap: No hard cap; keybox ban and in-person check-in rules debated
markets.tax: Contributo di soggiorno per night by category
markets.verdict: CIN code and SCIA filing required; enforcement debates (keyboxes, check-in) signal tightening.

markets.name: Florence
markets.registration: CIN code required; new STRs banned in UNESCO historic centre
markets.cap: Historic-centre restriction
markets.verdict: New STRs banned across the UNESCO historic centre — the core of the market is shut.

markets.name: Athens
markets.registration: AMA registration number; new registrations frozen in central districts (1st–3rd)
markets.cap: District freeze; national per-property limits
markets.verdict: Central districts frozen for new registrations; the rest of the city still registers.

markets.name: New York City
markets.registration: Local Law 18: registration with the Office of Special Enforcement (OSE) mandatory before listing; $145 non-refundable application fee
markets.cap: Stays under 30 days only in the host's own residence, host physically present, max 2 guests; unhosted short stays effectively banned
markets.tax: NYC hotel/occupancy taxes apply to legal registered stays; 30+ day rentals fall outside the STR regime
markets.process: Apply via the OSE registration portal ($145 fee); OSE checks the address against the Prohibited Buildings List and diverse dwelling-law criteria before issuing a registration number, which platforms must display. Class B dwellings approved for short-term occupancy are exempt from registration.
markets.whoCanHost: Only residents renting the unit they actually live in, present during the stay, max 2 guests. Investors and whole-apartment operators are out — 30+ day stays are the only legal route for them.
markets.zones: No zoning map — instead a Prohibited Buildings List: 14,000+ buildings (incl. all rent-regulated and NYCHA stock) have opted out; OSE must refuse registration there. Only ~3,000 registrations granted city-wide.
markets.penalties: Up to $5,000 per violation for illegal operation; the city has begun suing operators under LL18 (first suit May 2025) seeking penalties and disgorgement of illegal revenue. Platforms are barred from processing bookings for unregistered listings.
markets.enforcement: OSE actively enforces: platform booking-blocks at checkout, the prohibited list, and litigation. One of the hardest-enforced regimes in the world — assume the rules are applied as written.

history.change: City files its first lawsuit under Local Law 18 against an illegal operator — enforcement moves from booking-blocks to litigation.
Local Law 18 enforcement began: platforms must refuse bookings for unregistered listings; unhosted sub-30-day stays effectively end.

markets.verdict: The investor market is dead by design: ~3,000 legal registrations in a city of millions. Live-in home-sharing with two guests is all that remains under 30 days.

markets.name: Miami
markets.registration: City of Miami: zone-restricted; Miami Beach: heavily restricted with high fines
markets.cap: Zone-dependent; resort-tax registration required
markets.verdict: Zone-by-zone minefield; Miami Beach fines are among the highest in the US.

markets.name: Austin
markets.registration: STR licence required; Type 2 (non-owner-occupied) rules revised repeatedly
markets.cap: Licence + occupancy limits
markets.verdict: Licensed and workable, but Type 2 rules keep shifting — watch this one.

markets.name: Tokyo
markets.registration: Minpaku registration (juutaku shukuhaku jigyou) mandatory; notification number on listings
markets.cap: National 180-night/yr cap; wards add stricter weekday/zone limits
markets.tax: Accommodation tax per night (rate by price band)
markets.verdict: Legal since 2018 with registration, but the 180-night cap and ward-level rules make it a part-time business.

markets.name: Singapore
markets.registration: No STR registration exists for private homes — minimum stay is 3 months (private) / longer for HDB
markets.cap: Sub-3-month stays effectively illegal in residential property
markets.verdict: Effectively banned: three-month minimum stays make Airbnb-style hosting illegal in residential property.

markets.name: Bangkok
markets.registration: Hotel Act: stays under 30 days require a hotel licence most condos can't get
markets.cap: Daily rentals without licence are illegal (enforcement uneven)
markets.tax: VAT + local taxes on licensed operations
markets.verdict: Under-30-day rentals need a hotel licence; condo boards and courts back the ban — enforcement is the wildcard.

markets.name: Dubai
markets.registration: DET Holiday Homes permit required; operators and individual hosts both licensable
markets.cap: No night cap; unit standards + guest registration enforced
markets.tax: Tourism Dirham per bedroom per night
markets.verdict: Genuinely open: pay for the Holiday Homes permit, meet the standards, and operate without night caps.

markets.name: Sydney
markets.registration: NSW STRA register mandatory; property must be on the register to list
markets.cap: Non-hosted: 180 nights/yr in Greater Sydney; hosted: uncapped
markets.verdict: Registered and workable, but the 180-night cap on non-hosted stays halves an investor's calendar.

markets.name: Auckland
markets.registration: No dedicated registration; council targeted rates apply to full-time STRs
markets.cap: No night cap; business rates kick in above thresholds
markets.verdict: Open with registration-free hosting; the cost shows up as targeted council rates, not permits.

markets.name: Toronto
markets.registration: City registration mandatory; principal residence only
markets.cap: Max 180 nights/yr entire-home; principal-residence rule excludes investors
markets.verdict: Principal-residence-only kills the investor play; fine for renting your own home while away.

markets.name: Vancouver
markets.registration: City licence + provincial registration; principal residence only
markets.cap: Entire-home STRs outside principal residence banned province-wide (2024)
markets.verdict: BC's 2024 crackdown made this one of the strictest regimes in North America — principal residence or nothing.

markets.name: Mexico City
markets.registration: Mandatory registration on the city's Estancia Eventual platform (Llave CDMX login) — live since 22 May 2026; a folio must be shown on every listing and platforms (Airbnb/Booking/Vrbo) must verify it before publishing
markets.cap: Max 3 residential properties per host on the padrón; a 50%-of-nights occupancy cap remains legislated but contested
markets.verdict: Registration is live and a 50% night-cap is on the books — the rollout is messy but the direction is tightening.

markets.name: Cape Town
markets.registration: Zoning consent for non-primary use; tourism licence not required for small hosts
markets.cap: Max 30 days per booking in residential zones; no annual cap
markets.verdict: One of the friendliest big markets left: zoning consent, no annual cap, and the city wants the tourism.
